Spotlight: Usually ridden by Ciaran O'Shea; breakthrough win in 2m3f handicap at Fontwell last October and back-to-form third of 12 at Warwick (2m5f, good) early last month; in serious contention if that form can be maintained.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Has been in tremendous form at Hexham this summer, landing his fourth course win there last Sunday; incurs a 7lb rise and needs to show he can win away from his favoured venue.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Landed a gamble over hurdles at Kilbeggan (2m3f, good to yielding) in April following a 458-day absence and ran well when third off 14lb higher mark there last month; showed little in three runs over fences in 2024 for previous trainer but may be a different proposition these days; market watch advised.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: An infrequent winner but got off the mark over fences, after two placed efforts, at Hexham (1m7f, good) 15 days ago; cannot be dismissed while in this mood.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-9 but ran up to his best at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) last month when runner-up for second time over fences; first-time tongue-tie and hood are now on and he just needs to find that little bit extra to gain his first win.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Has three wins over fences but beaten a total of 70l on his two runs once resuming last month; has plenty to prove.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Irish raider who didn't fare too well in four attempts over fences previously, but got off the mark over hurdles at Kilbeggan (2m3f, good to yielding) last month at the 28th attempt under rules; not an obvious candidate to follow up, over fences.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Previously campaigned in hunter chases and points, he made an encouraging handicap debut when second to Ghasham at Hexham (1m7f, good) 15 days ago; may appreciate a longer trip.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Scored over C&D last August off 1lb lower; may have needed the run when fourth of six finishers at Bangor (2m1f, good) last month following six-month break and holds claims.

RaceName: Women's Community Matters Handicap Chase
Spotlight: At his best at Sedgefield where he boasts five wins; fair effort when third to Zumbi over C&D last month but it may be best to wait until he returns to his favoured venue.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins over 7f this year, two of them here, and he turned in another solid effort at Southwell 38 days ago (6f); no obvious reason why he wouldn't go well once again.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: C&D win in March off 3lb lower and she ran a better race when third of 12 in a Class 5 over C&D 18 days later; up and down since but she can't be discounted.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: He was winning for the 13th time when seeing off eight rivals over C&D last month; backed it up with a close second at Lingfield 24 days ago; more appealing than many.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: Stayed on from well back to win a 5f handicap at Chelmsford on her stable debut in March; beaten four times since and she is now going into new territory in terms of her stamina.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: Course regular; his latest third (8.6f) was a remote one behind an easy winner but it was still a creditable effort; no sign of age denting his enthusiasm and he should make another bold bid.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: 18-race maiden but there have been some near misses along the way; she was only sixth behind Split Elevens over C&D in May though and her losing run seems likely to be extended further.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: Flopped over C&D 51 days ago when reportedly unsuited by being unable to dominate; he had earlier won twice over C&D this year and he's not impossibly treated; goes without cheekpieces this time.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: 15-race maiden; neither of his 7f runs have been convincing in terms of stamina and there are more persuasive options today.

RaceName: Thanks To TC Titch & The Team From SFRacing Club Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner off 8lb higher in December; chased home Split Elevens here last month but not quite so good back here four weeks ago; apprentice takes off a useful 5lb; each-way shout.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Smart dual-purpose performer but on losing run since November 2024; excellent second in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket last October but well below best in three runs this term and this trip looks far too sharp; others appeal more.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: No joy since winning over 1m2f here (good) in September 2024; didn't fire last season and no sign of a revival in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel in sole run this year; remains 3lb above last success.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Down Royal maiden winner last June (10.5f, good); ran well in 1m2f handicap here last October and just 1lb higher here; shaped like coming back to form when keeping on from the back over 1m2f here latest; bred to stay this far; could go well.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Five-time winner at 1m2f-1m5f; quick ground fine and was beaten a neck when second in this race last year off 91; just 1lb higher now and shaped nicely enough on return from a break over 1m2f (good) here on latest; will strip fitter now and big player.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner added a second turf win in decisive fashion on return from 107 days off on first try over 1m4f (good) at Leopardstown last month; ridden by 7lb claimer that day so effectively 15lb higher here; will need a big career best to follow up, but unexposed at this distance and can't be discounted.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: 1m4f Roscommon maiden winner (good) shaped promisingly when a 1.5l second of 24 on handicap debut here in May (1m6f, good) off 10lb lower; not disgraced in Cork Group 3 (1m4f, good) on latest, but 6lb rise for that makes things tougher back in handicap company.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: In fine form this term; 1.25l runner-up on seasonal return over C&D (sft-hvy) in March; raised 4lb and won over 2m1f (good) at Killarney; good second off revised mark here on latest (1m6f, yielding); nudged up another 4lb and should run well again, but needs to find a bit more to win off this mark.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Won on handicap bow at Cork (1m4f, soft) last season off 14lb lower and then ran well in a Listed heat at Naas when a 3l second behind Carmers (1m5f, good); poor both starts this season, albeit heavy ground may not have suited on latest; could do better back on a sounder surface.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Won off 6lb lower here in October (1m2f, yielding); handles quicker ground and should come on for pleasing comeback run over 1m2f here (good) last month, but has to prove he stays this far.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Seven-time winner, including this race off 8lb higher in 2024; no joy last season but back to form with 2.25l second behind subsequent Royal Ascot winner Opportunity in class 2 handicap at Carlisle 29 days ago (1m3f, gd-fm); big chance if building on that run.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: In fine fettle this season, winning at Navan (1m2f, gd-yld) off 65 and on latest at Gowran off 78 (1m4f, yielding); hiked up another 9lb and moves up in grade now; unproven on properly fast ground, but this well-bred sort should go well if he acts on it.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Both wins have come at Down Royal, the most recent a fillies' handicap (1m5f, yielding) last September off 12lb lower; close fifth off this mark in Cork Derby (1m4f soft) on last Flat start; decent effort over hurdles at Punchestown 22 days ago; good ground fine and place chance back on the level.

RaceName: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Shock 125-1 winner of Navan maiden last season, then placed on handicap debut at Down Royal (1m5f, yielding); caught the eye making late gains over 1m2f here (good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (retained); eased 2lb for that 4.75l defeat (0.75l behind Cloud Seeker) and she's an intriguing runner for her in-form stable.
